source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure to create IAM roles for Backup

input_row.source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure to create IAM roles for Backup
input_row.description: An AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) role is similar to a user, in that it is an AWS identity with permissions policies that determine what the identity can and cannot do in AWS. However, instead of being uniquely associated with one person, a role is intended to be assumable by anyone who needs it.
input_row.rationale: While Service Linked Roles offer quick deployment, using default configurations isn't recommended for security best practices.
input_row.audit: To create a role for AWS Backup, follow these steps: 1. Navigate to the "IAM Dashboard" in the AWS Console. 2. Select "Roles" from the left-hand menu. 3. Click on the "Create Role" button. 4. Choose "AWS Service" as the trusted entity. 5. Select "AWS Backup" as the service that will use this role. 6. Choose a policy to apply to the role or create a custom policy. 7. Review the role details and provide a meaningful name for the role. 8. Click on "Create Role" to finalize the creation of the role for AWS Backup.
input_row.remediation: Assess your organization's needs to determine whether to utilize Service Linked Roles for AWS backups.
input_row.justification: While AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI) can be used to verify the existence and configuration of IAM roles for AWS Backup, the core of this control is the creation and initial configuration of a role tailored to organizational needs, including custom policies and naming conventions. This process requires human judgment and business context that cannot be fully automated, as it involves assessing permissions, selecting or crafting policies, and ensuring alignment with organizational security requirements.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to the IAM Dashboard. 2. In the left-hand menu, select "Roles" and click "Create Role." 3. Choose "AWS Service" as the trusted entity and select "AWS Backup" as the service that will use this role. 4. Select an existing policy or create a custom policy that grants only the necessary permissions for AWS Backup, based on your organization's requirements. 5. Review the role details, assign a meaningful and descriptive name that aligns with your organization's naming conventions. 6. Click "Create Role" to finalize. 7. Document the role's purpose, permissions, and any deviations from default configurations. 8. Confirm compliance by ensuring the role exists, is assigned to AWS Backup, and that its permissions match organizational policy.
input_row.default_value: When using the AWS Backup console for the first time, you can choose to have AWS Backup create a default service role for you. This role has the permissions that AWS Backup needs to create and restore backups on your behalf.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to the IAM Dashboard.
2. In the left-hand menu, select "Roles."
3. Review the list of roles to check if a role specifically created for AWS Backup exists.
4. If the role exists, click on the role name to view its details.
5. Verify that the trusted entity is set to "AWS Backup" under the "Trust relationships" tab.
6. Check the attached policies to ensure they are aligned with least privilege principles, granting only the necessary permissions for AWS Backup as per your organization's requirements.
7. Ensure the role name is meaningful and follows your organization's naming conventions.
8. Document the role’s purpose, any custom permissions, and any deviations from the default service-linked role configurations.
9. Confirm compliance by ensuring the role exists, is configured correctly, and matches organizational policy regarding AWS Backup.
